page: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_ni. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. There is no local storage or GPU hardware included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8a.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, placing in the top 10% of tested instances. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, along with image and text processing tasks, also rank in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance, cached small-block memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput fall into the top 25%. However, large-block memory bandwidth and database operations on Passmark register in the middle 50%, indicating potential bottlenecks during massive, uncached memory transfers. Small-model LLM inference achieves top-tier token generation speeds, whereas medium-model inference speeds drop to average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads, leveraging dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory without the premium cost of specialized accelerator hardware. The primary tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage, and average performance in large-block memory operations and database transactions. This server is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, text and image processing, single-threaded data compression, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.

It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU execution, image processing, and small-model LLM inference in the top 10% of performance tiers, while multi-core performance and static web serving throughput rank in the top 25%. Conversely, large-block memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. Lacking local storage, this instance presents a balanced resource profile for workloads such as static web serving, text and image processing, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core and image processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge features 16 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and Nitro virtualization, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for compute-intensive and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and image processing performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for static web serving, text processing, and small LLM inference
